title: Translation and Interpretation Services

narrative.executive_view: This packet is restricted to the frozen translation/localization-led archetype, not all NAICS 541930 firms. The selected archetype remains uncertain because the frozen alternative ranges overlap. S1 supplies market context but does not close the target-band accounting, implementation, or transaction evidence gaps.
narrative.revenue_and_pass_through: No target-provider revenue reconciliation identifies gross billings, net revenue, qualified agent-like pass-throughs, employee cost, and controllable freelance-linguist cost. S3 confirms contractor intensity historically, which is specifically insufficient to classify those purchases as pass-through or to create a value-added denominator.
narrative.technical_value: The frozen value basis is missing and its broad 5419 occupation mix is unsuitable for this archetype. S3's historical freelancer incidence and quality-review observations do not measure cash-cost weights or removable cash cost, so no V primitive, cost ID, or role-removability estimate is authored.
narrative.implementation: S1 describes automation and AI as an industry theme but also characterizes adoption as gradual and buyer quality expectations as material. It supplies no named target-band baseline, adoption cohort, annual realization endpoint, or investment schedule; all r and k primitives therefore remain missing.
narrative.commercial_capture: S1 reports market-level technology, price-pressure, and buyer-quality context, but it does not isolate retained savings after repricing for target-sized translation/localization providers. No source separates price, volume, service-mix, renewal, and switching effects by year, so commercial retention remains missing.
narrative.buyability: S1's survey reports broad LSP purchase and sale intentions, not a defined U.S. lower-middle-market translation/localization sale population, five-year offer rate, buyer-access probability, win rate, or completed-and-onboarded capacity. Those separately required buyability primitives are consequently missing.
narrative.entry_and_exit: No freshly accessed source provides closed, target-sized U.S. translation/localization transactions with all-in entry EV/normalized EBITDA, an entry-equivalent operating bridge, or downside exit evidence. The normalized year-five state and all multiple channels remain missing rather than being inferred from sector commentary.
narrative.operator_survival: S4 confirms that some communication settings require effective and qualified interpreting, while S3 reports historical quality-review practices. Neither source creates an exhaustive, point-identified translation/localization service basket reconcilable to controllable value added, so the constant-price operator-survival basket is missing.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: The central risks are archetype reclassification, absent target financial reconciliation, contractor-versus-pass-through treatment, unmeasured implementation and commercial response, and missing closed comparable data. S1's ranked global cohort is non-random and cannot cure the frozen target-band uncertainty.

scenario_narratives.low: No defensible low economic scenario is authored because the required denominator and the relevant r, k, c, B, P, and survival primitives are missing. The frozen coverage range is only a classification-enumeration input, not an outcome estimate.
scenario_narratives.base: The base record preserves the frozen selected archetype and its count-implied coverage point while withholding every unsupported economic primitive. It does not convert sector commentary, freelancer prevalence, or M&A intentions into a savings, return, or valuation conclusion.
scenario_narratives.high: No defensible high economic scenario is authored. A wider frozen classification range does not establish higher realization, retention, acquisition access, transaction multiples, or demand survival for a target-sized translation/localization-led provider.

sources.title: The 2024 Nimdzi 100
sources.what_it_supports: Freshly accessed provider ranking and survey context: listed language-service-provider revenues and service descriptions, plus reported 2023 M&A intentions. It does not establish target EBITDA, U.S. NAICS assignment, or closed transaction multiples.

sources.title: 2022 SUSB Annual Data Tables by Establishment Industry
sources.what_it_supports: Official source for firms, establishments, employment, payroll, and receipts by industry and enterprise size; it is the external population source used by the frozen band bridge, not evidence of individual-firm EBITDA or revenue mix.

sources.title: Review of the ALC 2015 Industry Survey
sources.what_it_supports: Historical U.S. language-company survey summary showing substantial freelancer use and quality-review practices. It lacks target-band cash costs, principal-agent accounting, and dated automation realization endpoints.

sources.title: ADA Requirements: Effective Communication
sources.what_it_supports: Official description of effective communication and qualified interpreter requirements for covered entities. It provides contextual evidence that regulated communication can have quality constraints, not a translation/localization provider demand or savings measure.

n_band.rationale: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $162,427 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541930); class <5: 2,193 firms, avg revenue/firm $268,027 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $41,946 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 223 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,388,377 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $217,281 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 120 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,092,642 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $483,998 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 95 firms, avg revenue/firm $5,796,705 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $907,184 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 28 firms, avg revenue/firm $40,892,000 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $6,399,598 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 15 firms, avg revenue/firm $211,163,133 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $33,047,030 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 28
caveats: Normalized dataset value lacks MED/HIGH support and is not promoted to DIRECT.

inclusion_criteria: U.S. business classified in NAICS 541930 and estimated to have $1 million to $10 million of EBITDA.

More than 50% of annual language-service revenue is generated by converting or adapting written material, including document translation, localization, terminology, editing, or closely related written-language quality services.
The business may also provide interpreting, but written translation and localization remain the majority revenue model.
exclusion_criteria: Businesses with more than 50% of annual language-service revenue from live spoken-language or sign-language interpreting.
Balanced providers for which neither written translation/localization nor live interpreting exceeds 50% of annual language-service revenue.
Software publishers, media-production or dubbing companies, language schools, individual freelancers, and internal language departments not primarily classified in NAICS 541930.
target_archetype.selection_basis: The 28-firm target-band total is fixed from the normalized dataset. Its 15.65% margin assumption implies a revenue screen of about $6.39 million-$63.90 million. The publicly disclosed 2024 Nimdzi ranking and just-below-ranking cohort contains 14 U.S. or U.S./China providers in that interval: seven described as translation/localization-led (MotionPoint, PGLS, Unbabel, Acclaro, Lilt, Willingjet, and Terra Translations), six described as interpreting-led (Certified Languages International, Linguava Interpreters, Hanna Interpreting Services, Language Link Corp, Translations in Motion, and Interpreters Unlimited Group), and one mixed provider (Akorbi). Scaling 7:6:1 to 28 produces base counts of 14, 12, and 2, so translation-localization-led is selected by the largest base count. The ranking is a non-random, incomplete provider list, includes verified and estimated revenue, and does not prove each firm's NAICS assignment or EBITDA; consequently the supported count ranges overlap and the selection remains uncertain.

recognized_revenue.rationale: No target-band trailing-twelve-month accrual recognized-revenue amount or target accounting reconciliation is available.
caveats: The frozen specification forbids inventing a controllable value-added denominator.

pass_through_reconciliation.rationale: No documented target pass-through category or reconciliation is available.
caveats: Empty partitions are the canonical structural treatment when no pass-through items are authored.

employee_cash_cost.rationale: The frozen value basis is missing and no target-band employee cash-cost amount is available.
caveats: Do not use the contaminated broad-industry occupation mix.

controllable_contractor_cash_cost.rationale: No target-band controllable freelance-linguist cash-cost amount is available.
caveats: Freelancer prevalence does not identify controllable cash cost or pass-through treatment.

r1.rationale: No target-archetype year-one realization endpoint is available.

c2.rationale: No year-two retained-savings endpoint separated from repricing and volume is available.

target_archetype_coverage.rationale: Preserves the frozen selected-archetype count bridge: 14 selected providers divided by the normalized 28-firm EBITDA band, with frozen low/high counts of 7 and 21. S1 supplies the incomplete ranked provider cohort and S2 supplies the industry-size data source used by the frozen population normalization.
caveats: The ranked cohort is non-random, contains verified and estimated revenue, and does not prove NAICS assignment or EBITDA for each provider.
Classification uncertainty remains because the frozen translation/localization and interpreting ranges overlap.

bridge.population: Nimdzi 2024 ranked and just-below-ranked language-service-provider cohort scaled to the frozen normalized 28-firm target band
bridge.geography: United States, with the frozen cohort caveat for one United States/China provider
bridge.period: 2023 provider revenue reported in the 2024 ranking; 2022 SUSB population data
bridge.unit: share of the frozen estimated $1 million-$10 million EBITDA band
bridge.business_model: translation/localization-led providers, with imperfect service labels and no firm-level EBITDA confirmation

five_year_sale_availability.rationale: No defined target-band U.S. offered-for-sale population or five-year observation exists.
caveats: Broad stated M&A intentions are not sale availability.

buyer_access_win_share.rationale: No target-band buyer-access and conditional signed-win evidence exists.
caveats: Access/win must remain separate from sale availability and price.

deal_execution_capacity_5y.rationale: No evidence establishes completed five-year deal capacity for the specified reference buyer.

integration_onboarding_capacity_5y.rationale: No evidence establishes accountable five-year full-onboarding capacity for the specified reference buyer.

buy_mult.rationale: No closed comparable supports an all-in entry EV/normalized EBITDA multiple for this target archetype.
caveats: Do not infer a multiple from ranking revenue or M&A intentions.

normalized_y5_operating_state.rationale: No source supports a normalized entry-equivalent operating state for low, base, and high multiple analysis.
caveats: Without a state, no state digest or entry-equivalent anchor can be created.

downside_exit_mult.rationale: Downside multiple state or allowed-channel evidence is unsupported.

cva_reconciliation.controllable_value_added_boundary: For this archetype, controllable value added would equal recognized customer revenue less only empirically documented third-party amounts that are contractually passed through to the customer, separately invoiced or otherwise demonstrably agent-like, and outside the operator's control. Freelance linguist and interpreter purchases are not presumed pass-through: they remain controllable contractor cash cost unless target-specific principal-versus-agent and cost-control evidence proves otherwise. Because no target-band reconciliation identifies qualifying pass-through amounts, R_cva and its employee/controllable-contractor cash-cost components are MISSING.

pass_through_reconciliation.rationale: No documented target pass-through category or reconciliation is available.
caveats: Empty partitions are the canonical structural treatment when no pass-through items are authored.

cross_checks.target_identity_preserved: The packet addresses only the frozen translation/localization-led archetype and retains its uncertainty.
cross_checks.pass_through_removed_once: No pass-through item is authored; the required empty GROSS_OF_ALL reconciliation has two empty partitions.
cross_checks.removability_only_in_v: No removability or role judgment is authored because the frozen value basis is missing.
cross_checks.investment_only_in_i: Investment schedules are missing and no investment has been embedded in commercial retention.
cross_checks.commercial_only_in_c: Commercial-retention schedules are missing and no price or demand effect is embedded elsewhere.
cross_checks.buyer_access_separate_from_price: Sale availability, access/win, capacities, and entry price remain separately missing.
cross_checks.p_uses_frozen_y5_state: The state, anchor, and downside multiple bind null consistently because no normalized state is supported.
cross_checks.p_excludes_c_and_survival: The closed multiple exclusion map excludes commercial retention and constant-price survival.
cross_checks.survival_is_constant_price_value_added: The required fixed-base CVA assertions are present, but the basket is missing because its reconciliation cannot be supported.

top_uncertain_inputs.input: Controllable value-added revenue and cash costs
top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: Without a reconciled denominator and controllable employee/contractor cash costs, no savings value can be calculated.

top_uncertain_inputs.input: Implementation realization, investment, and commercial retention
top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: No supported implementation or commercial-capture outcome can be assessed.

top_uncertain_inputs.range: Sale availability, access/win, capacities, and multiples missing
top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: No evidence-backed acquisition feasibility or price resilience conclusion is possible.
Overlapping provider-model ranges limit generalization even before economic evidence is considered.

reviewer_note: Fresh research was recorded only where it supports the stated contextual and frozen enumeration bridge. No unsupported primitive was converted into an estimate.
